steps.title: Spot the paradox
steps.content: The signal: “even when the green option was cheaper.” Calculation Mode aims to minimize financial cost, yet these participants selected the more expensive standard plan—a contradiction that requires explanation.

steps.title: Explain the decision
steps.content:
Answer C explains the paradox: the green plan imposes a burden (capping usage = inconvenience) that these participants weighed against the financial savings, and concluded inconvenience dominated.

The other choices either misattribute to social factors (B), assume they ignored financial data (A), or suggest doubt about the costs (D)—none fitting a two-factor decision model.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
